2024-09-20.thesis: Microsoft signs deal with Constellation to restart Three Mile Island plant
2024-09-20.exit_case: The Microsoft-CEG power purchase agreement was formally signed today; the deal announcement is now public and the initial CEG pop is already in.
2024-09-20.assessment: Entry week. Microsoft signed a 20-year power purchase agreement with Constellation Energy to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1, targeting ~2028. CEG and nuclear-adjacent names popped sharply on the news. The deal signing is the ENTRY TRIGGER, not the exit: the TMI plant has NOT been restarted — that is the substantive thesis event. CONCRETE pending catalysts remain: (1) NRC restart-license application and approval (filing expected near-term, ruling years out), (2) physical plant restart ~2028, (3) first power delivery under the PPA. The broader nuclear-for-AI-data-center theme is demonstrably early-stage — this is the first major corporate PPA to resuscitate a shuttered U.S. nuclear plant; follow-on deals from other hyperscalers are plausible but not yet announced. Coverage is heavy today but the arc is far from complete. CEG is the cleanest pure play: it owns and will operate TMI, captures the full power-price upside, and has no dilutive development-stage risk. Hold while the NRC process and restart timeline play out.
2024-09-20.exit_advice: Hold through NRC restart-license approval and physical TMI Unit 1 restart milestones; exit immediately if NRC denies the restart application, Microsoft cancels the PPA, or Constellation withdraws from the project.

Re-enter with a fresh entry when one of those becomes a datable, concrete trigger: e.g., NRC formally accepts the restart license application, a follow-on hyperscaler nuclear PPA is announced, or the NRC issues its ruling.

2024-11-08.thesis: Trump mass-deportation executive order expands ICE detention
2024-11-08.exit_case: GEO stock likely surged immediately on election night; the entire policy tailwind may already be priced in.
2024-11-08.assessment: Week 1 / Entry week. Trump won the election on Nov 5 but has not been inaugurated; no executive order has been signed; no detention-capacity expansion has been contracted or appropriated. The CATALYST (the executive order itself) is still entirely PENDING — the election win is the enabling precondition, not the resolution. Concrete pipeline still ahead: inauguration Jan 20, expected Day-1 immigration executive orders, subsequent DHS/ICE contract awards to private detention operators. GEO Group is the largest private ICE detention contractor and the cleanest direct vehicle — revenue is mechanically tied to bed-nights under federal contract. Thesis is early-stage and live. The devil's advocate price-in risk is real (GEO almost certainly gapped up on election night) but a stock re-rating on an election result is not the same as the policy arc completing; the actual contract and capacity buildout is months of execution still ahead.
2024-11-08.exit_advice: Exit when the mass-deportation executive order is formally signed AND ICE detention-expansion contracts/appropriations are publicly announced — that is the specific catalyst resolving. Do not exit on pre-inauguration silence or on the stock having already run.

With the first concrete execution gate now ~10 days out, silence continues to be the expected pre-execution state. Hold through silence into the inauguration window.

2025-01-24.thesis: Trump pledges US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve as named federal policy
2025-01-24.exit_case: Trump's inauguration crypto EO directed a working-group study of a reserve—not formal enactment; the most-hyped Day-1 window closed without the specific pledge being fulfilled.
2025-01-24.assessment: Week 11 — the inauguration window has passed and the specific catalyst has entered a formal process phase. Trump's Jan 23 crypto EO ('Strengthening American Leadership in Digital Financial Technology') is the most significant development in this arc: it (1) banned CBDCs, (2) established a Presidential Working Group on Digital Asset Markets chaired by the White House Crypto/AI Czar, and (3) explicitly directed the working group to evaluate creating a 'National Digital Asset Stockpile,' with Bitcoin as the primary asset under consideration. That is real, concrete forward motion — not speculation — but it is emphatically NOT the formal enactment of a US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve as named federal policy. The devil's advocate case this week is the sharpest in the journal: the most-anticipated Day-1 crypto window in recent presidential history closed without the reserve being formally established; the timeline has extended to roughly 180 days (working group report due ~June–July 2025); and markets appear to have sold the EO news (Yahoo notes 'Bitcoin bounces back,' implying a prior dip on the news). The 'Trump backs off key promise' headline causing a global rally refers to tariff rollback, not the Bitcoin reserve — off-thesis. Against the exit: the catalyst has NOT resolved (no formal reserve enacted), the driving condition has NOT reversed (Trump is explicitly continuing toward the reserve through a formal White House process), and the arc is NOT complete (a concrete, scheduled next step — the working group report and subsequent reserve decision — is ahead with a known timeline). The specific 'Strategic Bitcoin Reserve as named federal policy' arc is more mainstream post-inauguration but has not reached full sell-side saturation on a completed arc; the outcome remains genuinely open. Conviction lowered to 6 on three counts: (a) immediate Day-1 window passed without full catalyst, (b) thesis horizon now extends to mid-2025, (c) coverage is noticeably more mainstream. MSTR remains the cleanest vehicle — ~400k+ BTC on a leveraged corporate balance sheet, a mechanical amplifier of any formal supply-shock from a reserve program. Hold through the working group process.
2025-01-24.exit_advice: Exit if the working group formally recommends against a Bitcoin Reserve, or if Trump publicly abandons the pledge; hold through the 180-day process toward a formal reserve decision.

2025-02-28.thesis: Trump pledges US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve as named federal policy
2025-02-28.exit_case: Bitcoin's biggest weekly crash since FTX 2022, with press explicitly citing Trump 'crypto policy uncertainty' as a driver.
2025-02-28.assessment: Week 16 — sixth consecutive quiet week post-inauguration EO, and this week the noise is loud but off-thesis. Bitcoin's biggest weekly fall since the FTX collapse (5%+ to a 3.5-month low) dominates headlines. Press attributes the drop to two drivers: (1) Trump tariff uncertainty spilling into risk assets broadly, and (2) a $1.5 billion hack at a rival crypto exchange (Bybit) — neither of which represents a reversal of the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve policy arc. The 'crypto policy uncertainty' cited is macro-level sentiment, not a formal abandonment, rescission, or working group dissolution. The devil's advocate case is its most market-visible version yet: a crash of this magnitude signals that whatever reserve premium was embedded in BTC prices may be actively unwinding, and 'uncertainty about Trump's crypto policy' could include declining probability of the reserve itself. But the framework is clear — price-action-based probability repricing is not an exit trigger; a policy reversal is. The driving condition has NOT reversed: the Presidential Working Group on Digital Asset Markets retains its explicit mandate to evaluate a National Digital Asset Stockpile, the Crypto/AI Czar is still in post, and the ~June–July 2025 report remains a concrete, scheduled anchor. No public abandonment, no legislation killed, no working group disbanded. Per the framework, silence (and even a price crash) while a CONCRETE next step remains on calendar is hold territory. The arc is not complete. MSTR (~400k+ BTC on a leveraged corporate balance sheet) remains the cleanest mechanical amplifier of any formal reserve supply-shock, though its leveraged structure means the price damage this week is severe. Conviction stays at 6: Day-1 window passed without full catalyst, horizon extends to mid-2025, generalist coverage elevated, and now a significant BTC drawdown. Hold through the working group process into the report window.
2025-02-28.exit_advice: Hold. The crash is attributed to tariff uncertainty and a $1.5B exchange hack (Bybit), not a policy reversal. 'Crypto policy uncertainty' in the press framing refers to macro/tariff overhang, not a formal abandonment of the reserve arc. The White House working group retains its explicit Bitcoin stockpile mandate; the ~June–July 2025 report remains a concrete, scheduled milestone on the calendar. A violent price move is not a catalyst resolution. Exit only if the working group is disbanded, the EO mandate is publicly rescinded, or the report issues with a formal 'no reserve' finding.
